title: Flutter StreamBuilder rebuilding entire list
titleAr: Flutter StreamBuilder إعادة بناء القائمة بالكامل
desc: Wrap each list item with a ValueKey tied to a stable id so Flutter diffs correctly instead of rebuilding the whole list on every snapshot.
descAr: لف كل عنصر في القائمة بـ ValueKey مرتبط بمعرف ثابت ليتمكن Flutter من المقارنة بشكل صحيح بدلاً من إعادة بناء القائمة كاملة عند كل تحديث.
summary: Every Firestore snapshot triggered a full ListView rebuild, losing scroll positions, animation states, and expansion toggles. The fix is a single ValueKey per item.
problem:
Using StreamBuilder with Firebase Firestore, every snapshot caused the entire ListView to rebuild. Each item lost its scroll position, animation controller, and expansion state because Flutter's diff algorithm had no stable key to match old vs new children.

This is especially painful with real-time listeners that fire multiple times per second. Users see the whole list flicker, expanded cards collapse, and scroll position resets to top on every data change.
problemTitle: Entire list rebuilds on every snapshot
solution: Assign a ValueKey(item.id) to every list item widget. Flutter's Element tree uses keys to match old and new Widget instances, so adding one item no longer recreates all siblings. Scroll position and state are preserved across snapshot changes. The same principle applies to any builder that produces a dynamic list: always provide stable keys based on your data model's unique identifier, never the loop index.
solutionTitle: Provide stable keys to every list item

callout.text: Key idea: keys are not just for performance — they preserve widget state between rebuilds. Without keys, Flutter treats the list as a brand-new collection every time.

code.html: const key = ValueKey(item.id); // stable key

codeSteps.label: After — keys preserve state
codeSteps.html:
children: snap.data!.docs
    .map((d) => MyItem(key: ValueKey(d.id), data: d))
    .toList()

before.desc: Every Firestore snapshot rebuilds the entire list.

after.desc: Only added/removed items rebuild. Scrolling and state preserved.
